stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Beowulf is an Old English epic poem from early medieval Anglo-Saxon England.
  • In Beowulf, the character Grendel is often portrayed as a monstrous villain.
  • Grendel is a 1971 novel by John Gardner.
  • The novel is a retelling of the epic poem from Grendel’s point of view.
  • It often portrays Grendel as a misunderstood, philosophical outsider.
stem: The student wants to emphasize whose point of view the novel is told from. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: From the point of view of Beowulf, the character Grendel is often a villain.
John Gardner often portrays the character as a misunderstood philosophical outsider.
Beowulf is an epic poem that features the character Grendel.
Grendel is a retelling of Beowulf from the character Grendel’s point of view.

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The sentence emphasizes whose point of view the novel Grendel is told from: the character Grendel’s.

Choice A is incorrect. While the sentence discusses point of view, it focuses on that of the epic poem rather than the novel. Choice B is incorrect. While the sentence seems to be referring to Gardner’s novel, it doesn’t establish whose point of view the novel is told from. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence discusses the character Grendel in the context of the epic poem; it doesn’t discuss the novel’s point of view.
